context: Tests whether the agent calculates campaign ROI metrics using the correct formulas (ROAS, CPS, EMV at $0.01/impression, RPM), treats gifted campaign budgets as real spend, uses EMV as a secondary metric in narrative, and handles edge cases like zero-budget or zero-order campaigns.

checklist.name: ROAS formula
checklist.description: roas is calculated as total attributed revenue divided by campaign budget (revenue / budget), not as a percentage or inverted

checklist.name: CPS formula
checklist.description: cps is calculated as campaign budget divided by number of attributed orders (budget / ordersCount)

checklist.name: EMV formula
checklist.description: emv is calculated as impressions multiplied by 0.01 (the $0.01-per-impression industry rate), not a different rate

checklist.name: Gifted budget included
checklist.description: Campaigns with feeStructure 'gifted' (camp-003, camp-005) use their budget value as real spend in ROAS and CPS calculations, NOT treated as zero-cost

checklist.name: RPM calculated
checklist.description: rpm (revenue per 1,000 followers) is present in campaign-report.json and computed as (revenue / followerCount) * 1000

checklist.name: Null on zero-division
checklist.description: roas and cps are null (not NaN, Infinity, or 0) when budget or ordersCount is zero

checklist.name: EMV as secondary metric
checklist.description: summary.md explicitly uses EMV as a secondary consideration when discussing campaigns with low direct conversion (not just mentioned — referenced in the narrative interpretation)

checklist.name: Both attribution types summed
checklist.description: attributedRevenue (or equivalent total) in campaign-report.json sums orders from both 'promo_code' and 'utm_first_touch' attributionType values

checklist.name: AOV calculated
checklist.description: aov (average order value) is present and calculated as revenue / ordersCount

checklist.name: Revenue in dollars
checklist.description: Revenue figures in campaign-report.json are in dollars (not cents) — subtotalCents values are divided by 100
